response:
The living room LED output function is derived by subtracting natural light from the target, yielding \( L(t) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) \) when natural light is below 500 lumens, and zero otherwise since the sine wave peaks at 500.
blank blank blank blank blank blank blank blank
The total energy consumption over 24 hours is calculated by integrating the positive LED output function multiplied by the power rate of 0.1 watts per lumen-hour.
blank blank blank blank blank blank blank blank
The bedroom RGB combinations must satisfy the linear equation \( 0.3r + 0.6g + 0.1b = 500 \) with integer constraints between 0 and 255 for each variable.
blank blank blank blank blank blank blank blank
No valid integer solution exists for the bedroom because the maximum possible lumens achievable with full intensity (255 for all colors) is only 306 lumens.
blank blank blank blank blank blank blank blank
The system cannot reach the required 500 lumens in the bedroom using the specified color model and intensity limits without increasing the maximum intensity or adding more lights.
blank blank blank blank blank blank blank blank
Consequently the homeowner must revise the design specifications to include higher intensity LEDs or additional light sources to meet the 500 lumen target.
